# MedCMU-HPC

> MedCMU-HPC is a High-Performance Computing (HPC) service provided by the Faculty of Medicine, Chiang Mai University (CMU), Thailand. It is dedicated to providing cutting-edge computing resources for medical and health science research, including bioinformatics, computational biology, and deep learning workloads.

## Overview

MedCMU-HPC offers researchers access to a state-of-the-art HPC cluster, high-performance storage, and a wide range of pre-installed scientific software and containerized environments. The platform is designed to support demanding research workflows in medical and health sciences.

## Key Features

- **HPC Cluster**: A powerful research computing cluster designed for bioinformatics, medical research, and computational biology workloads.
- **High-Performance Storage**: Secure and scalable storage with dedicated project and scratch file storage, optimized for large dataset access.
- **Software Ecosystem**: Comprehensive pre-installed software including bioinformatics tools and deep learning frameworks, plus containerized environments for reproducible workflows.

## Installed Software

r/4.5.1-RStudio_Server_2025.05.1-513, dorado/1.1.1, ichorCNA/0.2.0, neofuse/1.2.2a, r/4.5.2-RStudio_Server_2025.09.2-418-Masslin2, fastp/0.23.4, idba/1.1.3, nextflow/24.10.0, rosetta/3.14

*Note on R and Apptainer Modules: Several modules, particularly R environments (e.g., starting with `r/`), are provided as Apptainer containers. Loading these modules sets the `$SIF` environment variable pointing to the appropriate container image. Users can easily enter the R interactive terminal or run scripts by executing `apptainer exec $SIF R`. This command is fully supported and recommended for use within SLURM batch scripts.*

### Micromamba Environments
GCparagon, MetaboAnalyst, MuPeXI, SqueezeMeta, abricate, annotsv, anvio-8, buildtest, cellbender, cellbender_v0.3.2, checkM, chewbbaca, circos, cnvkit, cobrapy, cpc2, crossmap_v0.7.3, deTiN, diffusers, dna_meth, ecole, ecole_cuda, emu-3.5.5, eye, feelnc, goatools, gtdbtk-2.5.2, hatchet, ichorCNA, igv-notebook, igv-reports, iqtree, isoseq, jupyter, maaslin2, marimo, maxbin2, mlst, nf-core, ollama, panaroo, picrust2, piggy, plek2, prokka, pytorch-v2.8.1, qiime2-amplicon-2024.10, qiime2-amplicon-2024.2, qiime2-amplicon-2025.7, r-ichorcna, raxml-ng, rosetta, s-tui, scons, snippy, sqanti3, talon, tama, varscan, vcf2maf, workshop_rared

## Useful Commands

- `sbalance`: Use this command to check your current Service Node Hours (SHr) credit balance.
- `myquota`: Use this command to check your project storage quota and disk usage.

## HPC Resources

MedCMU-HPC provides one open cluster for research staff and graduate students of the Faculty of Medicine, named **Raptor**. It became operational in November 2024 and consists of Dell compute servers powered by AMD CPUs.

### Raptor Cluster Partitions (SLURM)

| Partition | Total Cores | Cores per Node | Memory per Node | GPU per Node    | Max Time per Job | Max Cores per User |
|-----------|-------------|----------------|-----------------|-----------------|------------------|--------------------|
| compute   | 384         | 96             | 1,280 GB        | -               | 7 days           | 188 (Recommend max 94/node)* |
| gpu       | 192         | 96             | 384 GB          | 2x Nvidia H100  | 7 days           | 188 (Recommend max 94/node)* |
| short     | 6           | 1              | 4 GB            | -               | 8 hours          | 2                  |

*Note: It is recommended to use a maximum of 94 cores per node to reserve 2 cores for the operating system and general short jobs, ensuring fair use and system stability.*

- **short**: For short batch jobs, interactive jobs, array jobs, and multi-threaded jobs. Default memory is 4 GB per core.
- **compute**: For larger multi-node and long-running jobs.
- **gpu**: For GPU-intensive jobs.

### Hardware Specifications

| Node Type     | Number of Nodes | CPU per Node                    | Memory per Node | Cores per Node | GPU             |
|---------------|-----------------|---------------------------------|-----------------|----------------|-----------------|
| Compute Nodes | 4               | 2x AMD EPYC 9454 @ 2.75GHz     | 1,280 GB        | 96             | -               |
| GPU Nodes     | 2               | 2x AMD EPYC 9454 @ 2.75GHz     | 384 GB          | 96             | 2x Nvidia H100  |

Each node includes local SSD storage and runs Rocky Linux 8.10 with Linux kernel version 4.18.0.

- [Resources page](https://medcmu-hpc.netlify.app/resources)

## Organization

Faculty of Medicine, Chiang Mai University, Thailand
Website: [https://medcmu-hpc.netlify.app](https://medcmu-hpc.netlify.app)
